skill.name: modelarts-skill
skill.description: Integrates AI assistants with Huawei Cloud ModelArts for notebooks, training jobs, inference services, resource pools, VPC, KMS, SWR, and Lite Server operations.
skill.summary: Manage Huawei Cloud ModelArts resources from Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.
skill.author: modelarts-agent

security_audit.summary: Static findings were mostly false positives from Markdown backticks, identifiers such as id, and normal SDK wrapper code. However, the skill has high operational risk because it reads cloud credentials, signs outbound Huawei Cloud API requests, submits user-supplied training commands, and can delete or reset cloud resources. No prompt injection or confirmed malicious exfiltration was found.

high_findings.title: Cloud Credential Handling and API Signing
high_findings.description: The skill reads ModelArts access keys, secret keys, security tokens, project IDs, and regions, then creates signed sessions and requests. This is expected for the integration, but compromise or misuse would grant cloud account access.
high_findings.confidence_reasoning: Direct credential access is present and semantically tied to signed Huawei Cloud API calls. The code masks status output, so the risk is powerful credential use rather than proven exfiltration.

high_findings.title: Authenticated Cloud Resource Mutation and Deletion
high_findings.description: The skill exposes operations that can delete Lite Server instances, delete resource pool nodes, reset nodes, and manage authorizations. These are legitimate ModelArts administration functions, but they can cause service loss or privilege changes if invoked incorrectly.
high_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited functions perform authenticated destructive or privilege-affecting operations through cloud APIs. The behavior matches the skill purpose, but marketplace users need explicit trust and permission boundaries.

high_findings.title: Remote Training Command Submission
high_findings.description: The training job API accepts a command parameter and sends it in a ModelArts training job request. This is not local shell execution, but it can execute user-selected commands inside cloud training workloads.
high_findings.confidence_reasoning: The command parameter is intentionally forwarded to a remote training job configuration. Legitimate ML workflows need this, but it is still command execution in a cloud environment.

medium_findings.title: Dynamic Import Path Manipulation
medium_findings.description: Several modules modify sys.path before importing shared modules. The paths are local project paths and not clearly malicious, but this can increase dependency hijacking risk if the working directory is untrusted.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The pattern is real, but it appears to support modular local imports. Risk depends on whether users run the skill from a trusted directory.

medium_findings.title: Exec Login Information Exposure
medium_findings.description: The v2 inference helper can retrieve container exec login details, including endpoint, namespace, pod names, and cluster token metadata. This is useful for debugging but sensitive in shared or logged environments.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The docstring explicitly states that the API returns exec login information and cluster token-related data. The finding is contextual rather than evidence of malicious disclosure.

medium_findings.title: False Positive Static Pattern Groups
medium_findings.description: Most reported Ruby backtick, weak cryptography, C2 keyword, and Windows SAM alerts are not confirmed security issues. They come from Markdown code formatting, names such as id, API terms such as exec, and words like SECURITY.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: Targeted review found ordinary documentation and cloud API identifiers at the sampled locations. No local Ruby execution, weak cryptographic algorithm use, Windows SAM access, or prompt injection was found.

low_findings.title: Hardcoded Official Cloud Endpoints
low_findings.description: The skill builds Huawei Cloud service URLs for VPC, KMS, and SWR requests. These are expected service endpoints rather than suspicious third-party destinations.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The endpoints are derived from the configured region and point to myhuaweicloud.com services. Network access remains a risk factor because requests are authenticated.

low_findings.title: No Prompt Injection Evidence Found
low_findings.description: A targeted search for override, ignore-instructions, pre-approval, and similar prompt-injection language found no evidence in the reviewed skill files.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: No suspicious prompt-control text was found during targeted review. Confidence is not absolute because only relevant indicators and sampled files were reviewed.

dangerous_patterns.title: Signed Outbound HTTP Requests With Cloud Credentials
dangerous_patterns.description: The code constructs signed requests with access keys and security tokens for Huawei Cloud APIs.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: Multiple modules directly sign and send outbound requests using cloud credentials. The destination appears legitimate, but the pattern is high impact.

dangerous_patterns.title: Destructive Cloud Administration APIs
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill exposes delete, reset, stop, and authorization-management operations against ModelArts resources.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The operations are explicitly named and call authenticated cloud APIs. They are appropriate for an admin skill but dangerous if permissions are broad.

dangerous_patterns.title: Remote Command Workload Creation
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill can place a user-supplied command into a training job creation request.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The command is sent to a cloud training job, not executed locally. It still creates a remote execution surface controlled by the caller.

content.value_statement: ModelArts teams need fast access to notebooks, jobs, inference services, and cloud support resources. This skill gives Claude, Codex, and Claude Code a structured way to operate Huawei Cloud ModelArts APIs.
actual_capabilities: Authenticate with ModelArts notebook sessions or provided local credentials.
Manage notebooks, OBS storage attachments, images, tags, and leases.
Create, list, update, stop, and delete training jobs and inference services.
Inspect and manage dedicated resource pools, node pools, plugins, and node operations.
Query VPC, subnet, security group, KMS keypair, SWR image, and Lite Server resources.
limitations: Requires valid Huawei Cloud ModelArts credentials and matching IAM permissions.
Can perform destructive cloud operations, so least-privilege credentials are required.
Many calls depend on Huawei Cloud SDK availability and regional API support.
Does not replace cloud audit logging, change approval, or production runbooks.

use_cases.title: Operate ModelArts Notebooks
use_cases.description: Start, stop, inspect, and configure notebooks and related OBS storage during experimentation.

use_cases.title: Automate Training and Inference Workflows
use_cases.description: Create training jobs, inspect job status, and manage old and new ModelArts inference services.

use_cases.title: Administer ModelArts Infrastructure
use_cases.description: Review resource pools, node pools, Lite Server resources, VPC data, KMS keypairs, and SWR images.

prompt_templates.title: Check My ModelArts Status
prompt_templates.prompt: Check whether ModelArts authentication is available, then summarize the active project, region, and safe next steps.

prompt_templates.title: List Active Notebooks
prompt_templates.prompt: List my ModelArts notebooks in workspace 0, show their status, and identify which ones appear safe to stop.

prompt_templates.title: Prepare a Training Job
prompt_templates.prompt: Find suitable training resources and image options, then draft the parameters for a new production training job without submitting it.

prompt_templates.title: Audit Resource Pool Capacity
prompt_templates.prompt: Review dedicated resource pools, node pools, Lite Server capacity, and related VPC settings. Highlight risky operations before suggesting changes.

output_examples.input: Show my notebooks and which ones are running.
output_examples.output: A concise table of notebook names, IDs, statuses, workspace IDs, and suggested actions based on current state.

output_examples.input: Prepare a new training job for my custom image.
output_examples.output: A step-by-step plan that lists required image, pool, flavor, storage, command, and approval checks before creation.

output_examples.input: Review my resource pools for maintenance.
output_examples.output: A summary of pools, nodes, workloads, risky pending operations, and questions to confirm before any destructive change.

best_practices: Use least-privilege Huawei Cloud credentials scoped to the target project and workspace.
Ask for a dry-run summary before create, delete, reset, resize, or authorization changes.
Keep ModelArts, IAM, VPC, KMS, and SWR audit logs enabled for every session.
anti_patterns: Do not run this skill with administrator credentials for routine notebook tasks.
Do not submit training commands or custom images that were not reviewed.
Do not allow automatic deletion, reset, or authorization changes without explicit approval.

faq.question: Does this skill need Huawei Cloud credentials?
faq.answer: Yes. It needs ModelArts notebook credentials or local AK, SK, project ID, and region values.

faq.question: Can it create or delete cloud resources?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes create, update, stop, delete, reset, and authorization operations for several ModelArts resources.

faq.question: Does it expose credentials to the assistant?
faq.answer: The code attempts to mask credential status, but it still handles sensitive credentials internally.

faq.question: Is it safe for production accounts?
faq.answer: Use caution. Run it with restricted credentials, approvals, and audit logging because it has high-impact permissions.

faq.question: Which AI tools can use it?
faq.answer: The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.

faq.question: Why is the audit risk high?
faq.answer: The skill performs authenticated network calls, manages secrets, and can mutate or delete cloud resources.
